comment: Per-category product life-STAGE curves (MT12 NEW 1) — the time-based twin of the demand radar. typical_life_months = when a product typically reaches the end of its useful stage for the owner (a baby outgrows gear, a phone hits its refresh cycle). Used by lifestage.py to decide when to nudge the owner to resell BEFORE a buyer searches, with a forward value projection. Value + ₹/month decay are NOT baked here — they're derived from pricing.py over the real purchase date (auditable). asin_overrides re-key specific products to a more accurate stage (e.g. a baby monitor is baby-gear, not generic electronics).
